session.objective: Discover graph reasoning findings for maritime chokepoint risk

hypotheses.title: Single-chokepoint dependency can create concentrated country exposure
hypotheses.rationale: Rank country/chokepoint pairs by value share and dependent trade value to find countries exposed to one chokepoint.

hypotheses.title: Hazard severity should be joined to dependent trade value before ranking chokepoints
hypotheses.rationale: Combine hazard likelihood/severity with systemic risk results so the finding explains risk propagation, not just trade volume.

hypotheses.title: Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb escalation should prioritize dependent countries by systemic risk
hypotheses.rationale: Use the chokepoint hazard row and downstream country risk rows to prioritize analyst review when upstream events increase.

hypotheses.title: High throughput alone is not enough for a graph reasoning finding
hypotheses.rationale: A volume-only ranking does not explain hazard, dependency, country exposure, and action linkage.
hypotheses.pruned_reason: Pruned because it is a ranking/reporting hypothesis without a complete hazard -> chokepoint -> country -> risk metric -> action path.

candidate_findings.title: Bab el-Mandeb risk propagation identifies countries for immediate review
candidate_findings.conclusion: If Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb risk rises, the first review queue should include CHN ($15.1B at risk), IND ($7.1B at risk), USA ($6.6B at risk). The graph path is hazard at Bab el-Mandeb -> chokepoint -> dependent country -> systemic risk metric -> analyst action.

evidence_chain.value: Assign analyst review to top exposed countries and request updated live event enrichment.

evidence_limits: Draft candidate from maritime-risk graph playbook; requires human review before formal finding approval.
The playbook uses structural chokepoint risk data; ACLED/GDELT live events are a planned enrichment, not yet imported.

suggested_action.next: Create a Bab el-Mandeb review case for the top exposed countries and attach live event enrichment when available.

candidate_findings.title: Hazard-adjusted chokepoint risk should drive review priority
candidate_findings.conclusion: Taiwan Strait has the highest modeled trade-at-risk row in the current dataset: CHN shows $23.6B expected trade value at risk and $81.8B trade impacted.

evidence_chain.value: Create a priority review queue for countries with high trade_at_risk_v on this chokepoint.

evidence_limits: Draft candidate from maritime-risk graph playbook; requires human review before formal finding approval.
Hazard indicators are joined at chokepoint level; country-level risk is modeled through dependency and systemic risk tables.

suggested_action.next: Rank affected countries by trade_at_risk_v and validate current operational exposure.

candidate_findings.title: Single chokepoint dependency creates concentrated country exposure
candidate_findings.conclusion: GMB depends heavily on Gibraltar Strait: 75.9% of modeled maritime trade value flows through that chokepoint ($1.14B of dependent value).

evidence_chain.value: Prioritize dependency diversification review for the country/chokepoint pair.

evidence_limits: Draft candidate from maritime-risk graph playbook; requires human review before formal finding approval.
This phase uses structural 2022 dependency data and does not include live event updates.

suggested_action.next: Open a country/chokepoint dependency review and compare alternate maritime routes.
